steps.title: Read the intercept
steps.content: The graph crosses the $y$-axis at $12$, so the equation must have $y$-intercept $12$.

steps.title: Compare the slopes
steps.content: The line decreases from left to right, so the slope is negative. The graph falls $1$ unit for each $1$ unit to the right, so the slope is $-1$, not $-2$.

steps.title: Choose the matching equation
steps.content:
The equation with $y$-intercept $12$ and slope $-1$ is $y=-x+12$.
The answer is B.
